[[Category:Cases]]
The '''Speaker case''' is a unique case in Aelaki that indicates the source of information in non-visual evidential constructions. It marks the person who reported or conveyed the information being relayed.

== Formation ==

The Speaker case is formed by adding a ''-ro'' type suffix to the person-marked stem. Like the [[Agent]] case, the Speaker case has '''no inanimate form''', since uncountable/mass nouns cannot serve as sources of reported speech.

== Declension ==

Demonstrated with the feminine singular noun '''basol''' ("tree"):

{| class="wikitable"
! Person !! Form
|-
| 1st person || Basolthoro
|-
| 2nd person || Basoljoro
|-
| 3rd person || Basolshoro
|-
| 4th person || Basolkoro
|}

== Speaker Auxiliary Verbs ==

When the Speaker case is overwritten by another case (due to the [[#Case Hierarchy|case hierarchy]]), auxiliary verbs are used to preserve the speaker information. These auxiliaries vary by gender:

=== Child Gender ===

{| class="wikitable"
! Person !! Auxiliary
|-
| 1st person || Uthuro
|-
| 2nd person || Ujuro
|-
| 3rd person || Ushuro
|-
| 4th person || Ukoro
|}

The child gender forms are '''ironic''' in that they use reduplicated forms.

=== Female Gender ===

{| class="wikitable"
! Person !! Auxiliary
|-
| 1st person || Othoro
|-
| 2nd person || Ojoro
|-
| 3rd person || Oshoro
|-
| 4th person || Okoro
|}

=== Male Gender ===

{| class="wikitable"
! Person !! Auxiliary
|-
| 1st person || Atharo
|-
| 2nd person || Ajaro
|-
| 3rd person || Asharo
|-
| 4th person || Akaro
|}

== Case Hierarchy ==

The Speaker case holds the lowest position in the case hierarchy for verb agreement:

[[Agent]] > [[Dative]] > [[Patient]] > [[Instrumental]] > [[Possessive]] > '''Speaker'''

Because of this low ranking, the Speaker case is frequently overwritten by other cases, necessitating the use of the auxiliary verbs described above.

== See Also ==

* [[Agent]] - marks the subject of transitive/active verbs
* [[Patient]] - marks the object of transitive verbs
* [[Instrumental]] - marks the instrument, companion, or location
* [[Dative]] - marks the recipient or beneficiary
* [[Possessive]] - marks genitive constructions

[[Category:git synced pages]]
